channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says
item.description: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas' military wing, Israeli officials said, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.
item.content:encoded:
An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas' military wing, Israeli officials said, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.

(Image credit: Jehad Alshrafi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court is death knell for Virginia's Democratic-friendly congressional maps
item.description: The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get the issue on the ballot.
item.content:encoded:
The new map was drawn by Democrats and approved by Virginia voters, but the state Supreme Court declared the referendum null and void because lawmakers failed to follow the proper procedures to get the issue on the ballot.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Colorado's Democratic governor will let Trump ally Tina Peters out of prison early
item.description: Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.
item.content:encoded:
Gov. Jared Polis' controversial commutation follows a pressure campaign by the Trump administration to free Tina Peters, an ex-county official who was convicted of tampering with election equipment.

(Image credit: Scott Crabtree)
item.dc:creator: Bente Birkeland

item.title: Hantavirus countdown: U.S. cruise passengers settle in for 42 days of waiting
item.description: The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has potentially exposed passengers to a deadly disease. Most returning Americans are now housed in Nebraska. Some may be cleared to quarantine at home.
item.content:encoded:
The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has potentially exposed passengers to a deadly disease. Most returning Americans are now housed in Nebraska. Some may be cleared to quarantine at home. 

(Image credit: Dylan Widger)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Shakira returns to official World Cup song duty, this time with Burna Boy
item.description: FIFA's official 2026 World Cup song is "Dai Dai" from Shakira and Burna Boy. There are a number of factors that shape which songs define a tournament — and endure beyond it.
item.content:encoded: FIFA's official 2026 World Cup song is "Dai Dai" from Shakira and Burna Boy. There are a number of factors that shape which songs define a tournament — and endure beyond it.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: The Trump administration is planning a prayer event on the National Mall. All but one of the speakers is Christian
item.description: The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.
item.content:encoded:
The event comes as a new survey finds that many Americans aren't comfortable mixing religion and politics.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Post

item.title: Harvey Weinstein's third sex crimes trial in New York ends in mistrial
item.description: It was the second time in a year a jury was unable to reach a verdict on a rape charge brought by Jessica Mann.
item.content:encoded:
It was the second time in a year a jury was unable to reach a verdict on a rape charge brought by Jessica Mann.

(Image credit: Spencer Platt)
item.dc:creator: Chloee Weiner

item.title: 'The Wizard of the Kremlin' captures the rise of Putin — and 'anything goes' Russia
item.description: Set in Russia in the years following the fall of communism, The Wizard of the Kremlin doesn't always work dramatically. But you leave with a better understanding of how Vladimir Putin came to power.
item.content:encoded: Set in Russia in the years following the fall of communism, The Wizard of the Kremlin doesn't always work dramatically. But you leave with a better understanding of how Vladimir Putin came to power.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: Can the NFL's Rooney Rule survive the DEI backlash?
item.description: Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.
item.content:encoded:
Florida's attorney general says the NFL's Rooney Rule, which requires teams to interview minority candidates for top jobs, is discriminatory. Trump's EEOC has challenged such policies elsewhere.

(Image credit: Kevin C. Cox)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu
